children.label: A
children.text: any “local educational agency” (as defined in ) and an eligible employee of the agency; andsection 7801 of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: any private elementary or secondary school and an eligible employee of the school.

children.heading: Eligible employee
children.label: A Eligible employee
children.text: The term “eligible employee” means an eligible employee of an agency or school described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Employer
children.label: B Employer
children.text: The term “employer” means an agency or school described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Leave does not violate certain other Federal laws
children.label: b Leave does not violate certain other Federal laws
children.text: A local educational agency and a private elementary or secondary school shall not be in violation of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.), , or title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.), solely as a result of an eligible employee of such agency or school exercising the rights of such employee under this subchapter.20 U.S.C. 1400section 794 of this title42 U.S.C. 2000d

children.heading: Intermittent leave or leave on reduced schedule for instructional employees
children.label: c Intermittent leave or leave on reduced schedule for instructional employees
A
children.text: to take leave for periods of a particular duration, not to exceed the duration of the planned medical treatment; or

children.label: i
children.text: has equivalent pay and benefits; and

children.label: ii
children.text: better accommodates recurring periods of leave than the regular employment position of the employee.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 2 Application
children.text: The elections described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) shall apply only with respect to an eligible employee who complies with .section 2612(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: Rules applicable to periods near conclusion of academic term
children.label: d Rules applicable to periods near conclusion of academic term

children.heading: Leave more than 5 weeks prior to end of term
children.label: 1 Leave more than 5 weeks prior to end of term
A
children.text: the leave is of at least 3 weeks duration; and

children.label: B
children.text: the return to employment would occur during the 3-week period before the end of such term.

children.heading: Leave less than 5 weeks prior to end of term
children.label: 2 Leave less than 5 weeks prior to end of term
A
children.text: the leave is of greater than 2 weeks duration; and

children.label: B
children.text: the return to employment would occur during the 2-week period before the end of such term.

children.heading: Leave less than 3 weeks prior to end of term
children.label: 3 Leave less than 3 weeks prior to end of term
children.text: If the eligible employee begins leave under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of or under during the period that commences 3 weeks prior to the end of the academic term and the duration of the leave is greater than 5 working days, the agency or school may require the employee to continue to take leave until the end of such term.section 2612(a)(1) of this titlesection 2612(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Restoration to equivalent employment position
children.label: e Restoration to equivalent employment position
children.text: For purposes of determinations under (relating to the restoration of an eligible employee to an equivalent position), in the case of a local educational agency or a private elementary or secondary school, such determination shall be made on the basis of established school board policies and practices, private school policies and practices, and collective bargaining agreements.section 2614(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Reduction of amount of liability
children.label: f Reduction of amount of liability
children.text: If a local educational agency or a private elementary or secondary school that has violated this subchapter proves to the satisfaction of the court that the agency, school, or department had reasonable grounds for believing that the underlying act or omission was not a violation of this subchapter, such court may, in the discretion of the court, reduce the amount of the liability provided for under to the amount and interest determined under clauses (i) and (ii), respectively, of such section.section 2617(a)(1)(A) of this title
